what_it_does: This file registers Vi's identity as a declarative, editable system. It defines zone-specific facets, toggleable standing behaviour rules, a map of registry connections, and a catalog of renderable identity aspects. It persists user overrides in localStorage and exposes read, update, subscribe, and React-hook APIs.
observations: The module is an IIFE assigned to window.MS_IDENTITY.
Only identity overrides (behaviour toggles, text overrides, custom rules, facet edits) are persisted under the localStorage key ms_identity_v1.
Aspects are projections over existing registries such as MS_VI_BRAIN, MS_STREAMS, MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_FEEDBACK, MS_MEMORY, and MS_FEEDBACK_ACTIONS.
GROUPS defines the navigation sections of the Identity workspace.
FACETS maps zones to dispositions and leans, with a global fallback.
BEHAVIOURS is an array of built-in, always-on prompt rules that can be toggled or rewritten.
Custom behaviours live in overrides.custom and can be added or removed; built-ins can only be disabled or reset.
CONNECTIONS describes which single-source registries feed which identity aspect.
ASPECTS is a renderer-driven catalog keyed by projection type; adding an aspect makes it appear in the Identity surface.
Unknown ids for facets, behaviours, and aspects trigger console.error messages.
A React hook, useIdentityStore, subscribes to changes and forces re-renders.
Internal constants are exposed via _facets, _behaviours, and _connections.
standouts: Identity is composed as live projections over existing registries rather than a separate data store.
Facets let Vi behave differently per zone while remaining one continuous mind.
Behaviours are editable prompt rules that the brain reads every turn.
The ASPECTS catalog is extensible by projection type, so new identity views appear automatically.
conventions: IIFE assigned to a window global.
localStorage JSON persistence wrapped in try/catch.
Immutable-ish returns using slice, Object.assign, and spread.
Loud failure via console.error for unknown ids.
React hook named useIdentityStore with a force-update pattern.
concerns: Heavy reliance on global window objects with only optional existence checks.
load() silently swallows all JSON parse errors.
Custom behaviour ids are generated with Date.now plus a small random number, risking collisions.
setFacet accepts arbitrary patch shapes without validation.
Some read callbacks declare a ctx parameter but ignore it.
notes: This is the declarative identity registry that turns Vi's prompt personality, behaviour rules, and self-model into editable, persisted, renderable data.
questions: How does the Identity surface map each projection string to an actual UI component?
What is the exact shape of the ctx object passed to read functions?
How are the decision and decisions fields wired to writes in MS_DECISIONS?
Is MS_VARY a real registry, or is the leans aspect referencing a future/unimplemented one?
How does MS_VI_BRAIN select the active facet and fold behaviours into the prompt?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it makes identity a first-class registry, owns only override state, and supplies live projections for rendering and brain composition.
apparent_themes: Extensibility through aspect and projection registration.
intention_signals: It is trying to make Vi's identity inspectable and editable from one surface, so that changing rules or facets immediately changes how Vi behaves, and so the brain composes prompts from this registry instead of hardcoded strings.
novelty: It treats system-prompt personality and standing rules as user-editable, zone-tuned, persisted registry entries, and organizes them into a renderer projection catalog.
contribution: Provides a centralized, declarative identity model with facet, behaviour, connection, and aspect definitions plus override persistence; a fused system would keep this as the central place for Vi's self-description and prompt-composition inputs.
summary_for_embedding: identity.js is a browser-global registry that defines Vi's zone-specific facets, toggleable standing behaviours, registry connection map, and renderable aspect projections, persisting user overrides in localStorage and exposing subscribe/update APIs.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to make Vi's identity a first-class, editable, single-source system that the brain and Identity workspace compose from rather than hardcode.

symbols.does: Loads persisted identity overrides from localStorage.
symbols.description: Reads the ms_identity_v1 localStorage key, parses the JSON, normalizes the override object, and returns defaults if the key is missing or invalid.

symbols.does: Saves current overrides and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: Serializes the overrides object to localStorage under ms_identity_v1 and calls every registered subscriber callback so surfaces can re-render.

symbols.does: Registers a callback for override changes.
symbols.description: Adds the provided function to an internal Set and returns an unsubscribe function that removes it.

symbols.does: Merges persisted overrides into a facet definition.
symbols.description: Returns a copy of the given facet with disposition and leans replaced by any stored overrides, plus an edited flag indicating whether the facet has been changed.

symbols.does: Returns all facets with overrides applied.
symbols.description: Maps the FACETS object values through applyFacet so callers see live, user-edited dispositions for every zone.

symbols.does: Returns the facet for a zone, falling back to global.
symbols.description: Looks up a zone id in FACETS, applies overrides, and returns the global facet if the zone is unknown.

symbols.does: Persists a patch for a facet.
symbols.description: Merges the provided patch into overrides.facets for the given id and persists; logs an error if the facet id does not exist.

symbols.does: Clears persisted overrides for a facet.
symbols.description: Deletes the stored override entry for the facet id and persists the change.

symbols.does: Returns built-in and custom behaviours with overrides applied.
symbols.description: Merges toggle state and text overrides for built-in rules, appends custom rules from overrides.custom, and marks built-ins whose text has been edited.

symbols.does: Returns only currently active behaviours.
symbols.description: Filters the combined behaviour list to those whose active flag is true, which the brain folds into the prompt.

symbols.does: Locates a behaviour by id across built-ins and custom rules.
symbols.description: Searches BEHAVIOURS then overrides.custom for a matching id; used internally by update helpers.

symbols.does: Toggles a behaviour's active state.
symbols.description: Updates the active flag for a custom rule directly, or stores the toggle in overrides.behaviours for built-ins; logs an error for unknown ids.

symbols.does: Overrides the text of a behaviour.
symbols.description: Updates custom rule text directly, or stores the override in overrides.behaviourText for built-ins; logs an error for unknown ids.

symbols.does: Adds a new custom behaviour rule.
symbols.description: Generates a random id, pushes a new rule into overrides.custom with provided defaults, persists, and returns the new id.

symbols.does: Removes a custom rule or disables a built-in one.
symbols.description: Filters custom rules by id; for built-ins it switches them off and clears any text override, then persists.

symbols.does: Clears toggle and text overrides for a built-in behaviour.
symbols.description: Deletes the id from overrides.behaviours and overrides.behaviourText and persists the change.

symbols.does: Returns the registry connection map.
symbols.description: Returns a shallow copy of CONNECTIONS, the list of single-source registries and which identity aspect each feeds.

symbols.does: Returns the catalog of renderable identity aspects.
symbols.description: Returns a shallow copy of ASPECTS, each spec describing how the Identity surface should project it.

symbols.does: Looks up an aspect by id.
symbols.description: Finds the aspect in ASPECTS and returns it; logs an error and returns null for unknown ids.

symbols.does: Returns the UI group definitions.
symbols.description: Returns a shallow copy of GROUPS used to organize aspects in the Identity workspace.

symbols.does: Resolves a group id to its label.
symbols.description: Looks up the group in GROUPS and returns its label, falling back to the raw id if not found.

symbols.does: Organizes aspects by their group id.
symbols.description: Builds a map keyed by group id containing the aspects assigned to each group.
